name: DOGE 4h Value Recovery Scale-In v1
edge_mechanism: price flushes away from value, reclaims value, confirmation improves across bars, and exposure is built in stages instead of one-shot timing
thesis: The cycle still wants scale-in around value recovery when confirmation improves across bars instead of one-shot all-in timing. DOGE 4h remains the best-fit primary lane because it is live transition and should produce repeated flush-and-recovery sequences without the anti-correlated behavior seen in prior TAO continuation families. The edge is adding only as recovery proves itself.
invalidation_condition: If DOGE 4h value-recovery sequences still undertrade or staged adds mostly average into failed bounces, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price flushes away from short-term value, reclaims it, confirms that reclaim across successive bars, and resumes away from the recovery zone.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Transition markets often punish one-shot bounce timing but reward adding into improving recovery. Scaling in after value reclaim should improve location and reduce premature full-size entries.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: DOGE 4h is the best fit because it is the cycle primary lane, structurally transitional, and more likely than ETH 4h to show recovery continuation after a flush.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Staged entries may still build into weak mean-reversion bounces that fail before true continuation appears.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If count is healthy but expectancy is weak, reduce second-add size and bring the first partial closer before changing the trigger.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Repeated recovery attempts, many modest first-target wins, and fewer larger moves when recovery becomes true continuation.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: DOGE 4h clears density and staged recovery materially outperforms one-shot recovery timing.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-15 trades, or repeated scale-ins that behave like averaging losers.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable because flush-and-recovery sequences recur in transition

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit transition lane for staged value recovery.
validation_basket.continue_if: DOGE 4h shows enough value-recovery sequences with scale-in improving net expectancy.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: DOGE 4h staged entries mostly build into failed bounces.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate participation with several recovery continuations.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same staged recovery logic survives on a flatter similar lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h retains a smaller but tradable recovery behavior.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h is too flat or too sparse to monetize.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Cleaner, smaller recovery continuations than DOGE.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same recovery logic survives a trend-contaminated lane without becoming late continuation buying.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h retains enough recovery structure with acceptable density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h confirms the concept is too transition-specific.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower count, stronger recovered continuations than DOGE.

long: Initial 60% size on first close back above VWAP
Add 40% only if next bar closes >= EMA_20 and low stays >= VWAP - 0.07 * ATR_14
short: Initial 60% size on first close back below VWAP
Add 40% only if next bar closes <= EMA_20 and high stays <= VWAP + 0.07 * ATR_14

long: Stop loss: 0.82 * ATR_14 below blended entry
short: Stop loss: 0.82 * ATR_14 above blended entry
